untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options strategy (diagonal spread with call buyback and long call hold) that requires tracking multiple option legs, strike prices, expirations, and cost basis — none of which are expressible using price/technical indicators alone. The core mechanics involve: (1) near-term call buyback at or below strike, (2) profit recovery calculation, and (3) long-dated call holding. These require options-specific data (implied volatility, strike selection, time decay, premium tracking) and multi-leg portfolio logic not available in the price-action indicator set. The signal is fundamentally a multi-leg options structure, not a price-based trading rule.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_111 is a covered/diagonal call spread strategy that cannot be reduced to entry/exit conditions based solely on underlying price and standard technical indicators. Backtest engine would require options chain data, strike selection logic, and premium/cost tracking to properly implement this strategy.
